Home → Demo Hub → Agent Registry

Canonical registry

Exact agent and department counts are now declared from project artifacts.

This page turns the structured export graph into a visible registry view. In this demo, an agent means registered command-center operating units from the static org-chart registry. These are not live autonomous processes. The source is static, read-only, and traceable. Live runtime agents remain disabled.

Exact agent count: 47,979 Exact department count: 1,777 Exact unit count: 5,331 Exact family count: 175 Hierarchy levels: 7 Operational domains: 11 Live runtime agents enabled: 0 Runtime activation: not started

Registry source

Registry source: generated from project artifacts. Generated from 09_exports/org_chart_export.json. Traceability: open static traceability page.

The count is a registry scale count, not a runtime execution count.

Runtime activation not started.

Registered vs runtime

  • Registered agents: 47,979
  • Live runtime agents: 0
  • Activation: not started
  • Meaning: the registry exists; automation remains disabled.

Why this matters

The value is not that 47,979 agents are running today. The value is that the command structure, safety model, registry, simulator, and review process are visible before runtime activation.

Families

175 family groups form the declared registry backbone.

Departments

1,777 departments are grouped under those families.

Units

5,331 units sit underneath the departments.

Agents

47,979 team-member agents are declared in the export graph.

Hierarchy levels

7 governance levels are documented.

Operational domains

11 operational domains are documented.

© 2026 Devin O’Rourke. All rights reserved.

Agent Command Center, the demo pages, registry presentation, dashboard copy, visual layout, simulator materials, validator descriptions, and stakeholder presentation materials are protected project materials. No license, sale, transfer, or permission to reuse is granted by viewing this demo.

This site is a static, read-only demonstration. It does not enable live automation, command execution, database writes, Supabase mutations, deployment actions, rollback execution, alert sending, or runtime activation.

Third-party names, platforms, and services referenced for context remain the property of their respective owners.
